title: Specifying log_tag in containers.conf overrides --log-opt tag=VALUE specified via command line
body:
### Issue Description

containers.conf says the following:

> <...> All of the options have hard coded defaults and these options will override
> the built in defaults. Users can then override these options via the command line. <...>

However, when specifying `log_tag` via containers.conf and via command line via `--log-opt tag=value`, the command line fails to override the option specified in containers.conf

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Add to containers.conf

```
[containers]
log_tag = "basetag"
```

2. Run `sudo podman run --name testcontainer --log-opt tag=overriddentag --rm debian:bookworm /bin/bash -c "while true; do echo abcd; sleep 1; done"`.

3. Observe logs via `sudo journalctl -f -o json -f | jq`. Note that CONTAINER_TAG will contain basetag value, not overriddentag.





### Describe the results you received

CONTAINER_TAG contains value from containers.conf

### Describe the results you expected

CONTAINER_TAG should contain value from --log-opt
